articles.title: 14 Killed in deadly clashes between Warrap and Lakes communities
articles.summary: The government of Warrap State says at least 14 people have been killed in a deadly clash involving communities from Warrap and neighbouring Lakes State. According to a statement issued by Warrap State Information Minister William Wol Mayom Bol, the violence erupted on Wednesday in the Thoony-door area involving Wun-adel and Wun-thuc communities of Tonj […] The post 14 Killed in deadly clashes between Warrap and Lakes communities first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The clashes between the Wun-adel and Wun-thuc communities in South Sudan's Warrap and Lakes States are rooted in longstanding ethnic tensions exacerbated by competition over resources and land. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to address such conflicts but has struggled to achieve lasting stability. Recent violence reflects the fragile security situation in the region, where local grievances often escalate into deadly confrontations, undermining national peace efforts.

articles.title: توجه لتدشين بنك للذهب في مصر
articles.summary: كشف رئيس مجلس إدارة البنك الإفريقي للتصدير والاستيراد “أفريكسم بنك” جورج إيلومبي، عن تعاون مع البنك المركزي المصري، لإنشاء بنك متخصص في الذهب يكون مقره القاهرة.وقال إيلومبي، في تصريحات صحفية خلال وجوده في مصر، إنه تم اختيار مصر لتكون المقر الرئيسي لبنك الذهب الإفريقي، لما تتمتع به من استقرار وأمن، بجانب المناخ الاستثماري المحفز والخبرات …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في ظل التحديات الاقتصادية التي تواجهها مصر والسودان، حيث يسعى البلدان لتعزيز استقرارهما المالي. كما أن إنشاء بنك الذهب يعكس جهود مصر لتعزيز مكانتها كمركز مالي إقليمي، خاصة بعد توقيع اتفاقية التجارة الحرة القارية الإفريقية في 2021، والتي تهدف إلى تعزيز التجارة بين الدول الإفريقية. في الوقت نفسه، يعاني السودان من صراعات داخلية تؤثر على اقتصاده، مما يجعل التعاون مع مصر أكثر أهمية.

articles.context: تأتي تصريحات ترامب حول "الاتفاقات التجارية الرائعة" مع الصين في وقت حساس، حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين الولايات المتحدة والصين بسبب قضايا التجارة والتكنولوجيا. في 2020، فرضت الولايات المتحدة رسومًا جمركية على السلع الصينية، مما أدى إلى حرب تجارية مستمرة. كما أن المحادثات الجارية بين لبنان وإسرائيل في واشنطن تشير إلى جهود دبلوماسية أوسع في المنطقة، مما يزيد من أهمية التعاون التجاري بين القوى الكبرى في ظل التوترات الإقليمية.

articles.context: تأتي هذه المقالة في وقت تشهد فيه السودان توترات سياسية متزايدة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. هذه الصراعات تعكس تاريخًا طويلًا من الصراعات الداخلية، مما يثير تساؤلات حول الاستقرار الإقليمي والدولي. كما أن ذكرى ظهورات فاطمة في البرتغال عام 1917 تثير اهتمامًا خاصًا في سياق الأزمات الروحية والسياسية التي يواجهها السودان اليوم.

articles.title: لولا الحرب وحرص بريطانيا على دور في السودان لقامت بطرد السفير ورئيس الوزراء

articles.context: تتزامن هذه الأحداث مع تصاعد النزاع في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى أزمة إنسانية كبيرة. في هذا السياق، تسعى بريطانيا للحفاظ على دورها في المنطقة، خاصة بعد أن أظهرت الحكومة البريطانية اهتمامًا بتحقيق الاستقرار في السودان. كما أن العلاقات بين السودان وجنوب السودان تتأثر بشكل مباشر بالوضع السياسي المتقلب، مما يزيد من تعقيد المشهد الإقليمي.

articles.title: Three children die in house fire in Aweil North
articles.summary: Police in Northern Bahr el Ghazal State have confirmed that three children, including two-year-old twins, died in a house fire in Shawuong Village of Malual Centre Payam, Aweil North County. According to Northern Bahr el Ghazal Police spokesperson Colonel Madut Ngong Aleu, the tragic incident happened at around 11:05 a.m. on March 13th after the […] The post Three children die in house fire in Aweil North first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The tragic house fire in Aweil North occurs amid ongoing challenges in South Sudan, where instability and lack of resources have heightened risks for vulnerable populations. The country has been grappling with the aftermath of the civil war that began in 2013, leading to widespread displacement and inadequate access to emergency services. Recent peace agreements, such as the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) signed in 2018, have yet to fully stabilize the region.

articles.title: الهلال السوداني يصبح أول فريق في العالم يفوز بلقب الدوري في 3 دول مختلفة

articles.context: تأتي إنجازات الهلال السوداني في سياق تاريخي معقد، حيث يعاني السودان من صراعات سياسية واقتصادية منذ الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019. في ظل هذه الظروف، يسعى النادي لتعزيز مكانته الإقليمية والدولية، خاصة بعد مشاركته في الدوري الرواندي هذا الموسم، مما يعكس التحديات التي تواجه الأندية السودانية في ظل الأزمات المحلية. كما أن فوز الهلال يعكس تطور كرة القدم في المنطقة رغم الصعوبات.

articles.title: 'Nightmare in Nairobi': Kenya's Onyango vows to floor Tanzania's Mchunguzi in big fight
articles.summary: Nairobi to host high stakes bout tonight with several undercard fights.
articles.context: The upcoming fight between Kenya's Onyango and Tanzania's Mchunguzi is set against a backdrop of rising nationalism in both countries, particularly following the 2022 East African Community (EAC) summit where regional tensions were highlighted. The bout symbolizes not only a sporting rivalry but also the broader competition for regional dominance in sports and culture, as both nations seek to assert their influence in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Why MPs want Nakuru State House relocated
articles.summary: Members of Parliament want the facility moved from its current location.
articles.context: The call to relocate the Nakuru State House comes amid ongoing tensions between local leaders and the national government over resource allocation and political representation. Since the 2010 Constitution, which devolved power to counties, Nakuru has been a focal point for disputes over land and governance. The recent push follows a series of protests in 2023 advocating for greater local autonomy and equitable distribution of national resources, highlighting the region's complex political dynamics.

articles.title: Born to teenagers: The children who grew up carrying their mothers’ choices
articles.summary: They reflect on sacrifice, resilience, stigma and unconditional love shaping their lives.
articles.context: In Kenya, the rise of teenage pregnancies has become a pressing social issue, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic, which saw a reported 40% increase in such cases in 2020. The government’s National Adolescent Sexual and Reproductive Health Policy, launched in 2015, aimed to address these challenges, yet stigma and limited access to education and healthcare persist. This context highlights the resilience of children born to teenage mothers, who navigate societal pressures while embodying their mothers' sacrifices.

articles.title: Mbadi vs State House: Storm over CDF board members’ recruitment
articles.summary: Leaked correspondence triggers major storm that could spill over to the courts.
articles.context: The controversy surrounding the recruitment of Constituency Development Fund (CDF) board members has intensified following leaked correspondence implicating key political figures, including National Assembly Minority Leader Opiyo Wandayi and State House officials. This dispute arises amid ongoing tensions between the opposition and the ruling party, particularly following the contentious 2022 general elections, which have left deep political divisions in Kenya. The outcome could set a precedent for governance and accountability in the management of public funds.

articles.title: 125 post offices shut in Sh1bn cost-cutting drive
articles.summary: Restructuring more meant to return the parastatal to profitability.
articles.context: The closure of 125 post offices in Kenya is part of a broader restructuring effort by the Kenya Post Office, which has faced financial challenges since the liberalization of the telecommunications sector in the early 2000s. With increasing competition from mobile money services like M-Pesa, the parastatal aims to cut costs and return to profitability amid ongoing discussions about public sector reforms initiated by the government in 2021.

articles.title: Court orders fresh mental assessment for man accused of murdering two patients at KNH
articles.summary: Kalombotole is accused of killing two patients at KNH in 2025.
articles.context: The case of Kalombotole comes amid heightened scrutiny of mental health care in Kenya, particularly following the 2021 Mental Health Act aimed at improving patient rights and care standards. The incident at Kenyatta National Hospital (KNH) has raised concerns about safety protocols in healthcare facilities, reflecting ongoing debates about accountability and the treatment of mentally ill individuals in the justice system. This case could influence future legislation and public perception of mental health issues in Kenya.

articles.title: Political thunder: Why the stakes were high in Emurua Dikirr poll
articles.summary: The political consequences of the by-election were always going to be profound.
articles.context: The Emurua Dikirr by-election, held on February 25, 2023, was a critical contest following the death of the sitting MP, Johana Ngeno, which left a power vacuum in the region. This election was particularly significant as it reflected the ongoing political rivalry between the Kenya Kwanza coalition and the opposition Azimio la Umoja coalition, with implications for the broader political landscape in Narok County, where ethnic dynamics and historical grievances play a crucial role.

articles.context: تأتي هذه المحادثات في وقت حساس حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين إسرائيل وحزب الله، خاصة بعد سلسلة من الهجمات المتبادلة في مايو 2023. الاتفاقات السابقة، مثل قرار الأمم المتحدة 1701 في 2006، لم تحقق استقرارًا دائمًا في المنطقة. كما أن الضغوط الأمريكية على حزب الله، الذي يعتبره واشنطن منظمة إرهابية، تعقد جهود التوصل إلى اتفاق شامل، مما يجعل هذه الجولة من المحادثات محورية في سياق الصراع المستمر.

articles.title: David Keter of UDA elected Emurua Dikirr MP
articles.summary: David Keter won the seat after garnering 18,266 votes.
articles.context: David Keter's election as the Member of Parliament for Emurua Dikirr comes amid heightened political tensions in Kenya following the controversial 2022 general elections, which saw the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) emerge as a significant political force under President William Ruto. The region has historically been a stronghold for the Kenya African National Union (KANU), and Keter's victory reflects a shift in local political dynamics and voter sentiment toward UDA's development agenda.

articles.title: Live blog: UDA wins Emurua Dikirr and Endo Ward seats in by-elections
articles.summary: David Keter won the Emurua Dikirr parliamentary by-election.
articles.context: The by-elections in Emurua Dikirr and Endo Ward come amid heightened political tensions following the 2022 general elections, where the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) emerged as a significant player under President William Ruto. David Keter's victory is particularly notable as it reflects UDA's consolidation of power in Narok County, a region historically dominated by rival parties. This win could influence upcoming political alignments ahead of the 2027 elections, reshaping local governance dynamics.

articles.title: UDA's Festus Kiprop wins Endo Ward by-election
articles.summary: Festus Kiprop garners 4,475 votes to win Endo Ward by-election.
articles.context: The Endo Ward by-election, held on October 5, 2023, follows a period of political realignment in Kenya, particularly after the 2022 general elections that saw the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) gain prominence under President William Ruto. Kiprop's victory is significant as it reflects UDA's consolidation of power in the Rift Valley region, which has historically been a stronghold for the party amid ongoing tensions between various political factions and the opposition coalition, Azimio la Umoja.

articles.title: “ود الأشبة” في جوبا… الفن لا يسأل عن الهوية

articles.context: The article highlights the cultural exchange between Sudan and South Sudan amidst ongoing tensions following the 2011 secession of South Sudan. articles.title: Let Macron eat from wife’s kitchen

Additionally, Kenya's recent participation in the African Union-led peacekeeping mission in Haiti has drawn scrutiny, highlighting the complexities of international partnerships. This backdrop underscores the growing sentiment among African leaders advocating for autonomy from Western influence.

articles.title: East Africa extraordinary week and sound of a new continent
articles.summary: This week, East Africa staged a diplomatic play without precedent in recent memory.
articles.context: This week’s diplomatic activities in East Africa come on the heels of the recent signing of the Nairobi Declaration on Peace and Security in the Horn of Africa on June 25, 2023, aimed at addressing regional conflicts, particularly in Ethiopia and South Sudan. Kenya's leadership role in mediating these tensions, coupled with its strategic partnerships with the African Union and IGAD, underscores its growing influence in fostering stability and cooperation in the region.

articles.title: Next health revolution must be led by nurses
articles.summary: Empowered nurses do more than save individual lives.
articles.context: In Kenya, the COVID-19 pandemic exposed significant gaps in the healthcare system, highlighting the critical role of nurses in managing public health crises. The 2020 Health Act aimed to enhance healthcare delivery but faced challenges due to inadequate funding and political instability. As the country grapples with ongoing strikes by healthcare workers demanding better pay and conditions, empowering nurses is essential for sustainable health reforms and improving community health outcomes.

articles.title: Why you need a beginner’s mind in retirement
articles.summary: Without purpose, learning and fresh energy, retirement can be empty and confusing.
articles.context: In Kenya, the rising life expectancy and increasing number of retirees, particularly after the 2010 Constitution expanded social benefits, have highlighted the need for meaningful engagement in later life. As the country grapples with economic challenges and a youth-dominated workforce, retirees face a unique struggle to find purpose and contribute to society. This demographic shift underscores the importance of adopting a beginner's mind to navigate the complexities of retirement in a rapidly changing environment.

articles.title: Citizen power antidote to buildings collapse
articles.summary: Recurrent cases of collapsing buildings point to a construction sector that has lost...
articles.context: In Kenya, the construction sector has faced scrutiny following several high-profile building collapses, notably the 2016 Huruma collapse that killed 51 people. As citizens increasingly advocate for accountability, the recent push for community-led oversight reflects a growing demand for transparency and safety in urban development, particularly in the context of Nairobi's rapid population growth and informal settlements.

articles.title: Enhance prevention to ease healthcare burden
articles.summary: There is a need to enhance preventive healthcare to ease the financial burden.
articles.context: Kenya's healthcare system has been under strain due to the COVID-19 pandemic and ongoing challenges such as high rates of non-communicable diseases. The government’s 2019 Universal Health Coverage pilot program aimed to improve access to healthcare, but implementation has faced setbacks. Additionally, the recent political tensions surrounding the 2022 elections have diverted attention and resources away from healthcare initiatives, highlighting the urgent need for enhanced preventive measures to alleviate the financial burden on the system.

articles.title: Stop neglect of boy child
articles.summary: The boys’ plight is increasingly worsening.
articles.context: In Kenya, the ongoing challenges faced by boys, particularly in marginalized communities, have been exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic, which led to increased school dropouts and rising rates of child labor. The 2019 Kenya Demographic and Health Survey highlighted alarming statistics on gender-based violence and mental health issues among boys. Additionally, the government's focus on girls' education through initiatives like the 2016 National Strategy for Girls' Education has inadvertently sidelined the needs of boys, prompting urgent calls for attention.

articles.title: Why Sarah Mtalii wants her 50th birthday to be her wedding day

articles.title: Truth, trust dominate Africa media summit

articles.context: تشهد السودان صراعًا مستمرًا منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، حيث تصاعدت حدة القتال بعد انهيار اتفاقيات السلام السابقة. في عام 2021، أدى الانقلاب العسكري الذي قاده الجنرال عبد الفتاح البرهان إلى تفاقم الأوضاع، مما ساهم في تفشي العنف والقتل بين المدنيين. تزايدت الهجمات الجوية مؤخرًا، مما يسلط الضوء على تفاقم الأزمات الإنسانية في البلاد.

articles.title: دراسة التمكين السياسي في السودان: إشكالات نظرية ومنهجية (2-3)

articles.context: تأتي هذه الدراسة في ظل الأزمات السياسية المستمرة في السودان، خاصة بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في أبريل 2019، حيث شهدت البلاد صراعات بين الجيش وقوى الحرية والتغيير. كما أن اتفاق جوبا للسلام في أكتوبر 2020 لم يحقق الاستقرار المنشود، مما زاد من تعقيد المشهد السياسي. تظل الهيمنة العسكرية على السلطة والتحديات الاقتصادية والاجتماعية من أبرز القضايا التي تواجه السودان اليوم.

articles.title: Ruto grants university charters to KAIST and KEMRI
articles.summary: President Ruto said the two institutions would serve as strategic pillars in Kenya’s...
articles.context: The granting of charters to the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) and the Kenya Medical Research Institute (KEMRI) comes amid Kenya's push to enhance its educational and research capabilities, particularly following the 2021 Kenya-Korea bilateral agreement aimed at strengthening cooperation in science and technology. This move aligns with President Ruto's agenda to position Kenya as a regional leader in innovation, especially in the wake of ongoing challenges in healthcare and education exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: مصر تعلن عن ثورة رقمية في مطاراتها لأول مرة
articles.summary: شهد رئيس الوزراء المصري مصطفى مدبولي توقيع اتفاقيتي إدارة وتشغيل المنظومة الرقمية المستحدثة للحصول على التأشيرة عند الوصول بمطار القاهرة الدولي. ويأتي هذا المشروع الضخم بالتعاون بين شركة “ساي شيلد للتكنولوجيا” المنفذة، وبنكي الأهلي ومصر كمقدمين للخدمة، وبحضور وزير السياحة والآثار وممثلين عن البنك المركزي المصري، ليعلن رسمياً عن بدء حقبة جديدة في تيسير إجراءات …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة في إطار جهود مصر لتعزيز السياحة وجذب الاستثمارات الأجنبية، خاصة بعد تأثير جائحة كورونا على القطاع. كما تتزامن مع التوترات الإقليمية في منطقة القرن الأفريقي، حيث تسعى مصر لتعزيز علاقاتها مع الدول المجاورة، مثل السودان، في ظل التحديات السياسية والاقتصادية. توقيع هذه الاتفاقيات في 2023 يعكس التزام الحكومة المصرية بتحديث البنية التحتية وتعزيز الأمن في مطاراتها.

articles.context: تتجدد التوترات بين تركيا وإيران في سياق الصراعات الإقليمية المتزايدة، خاصة بعد تصاعد النفوذ الإيراني في العراق وسوريا. منذ عام 2011، أدت الحرب الأهلية السورية إلى تعميق الخلافات بين أنقرة وطهران، حيث تدعم تركيا جماعات معارضة بينما تدعم إيران النظام. كما أن الاتفاق النووي الإيراني لعام 2015 زاد من تعقيد العلاقات، حيث تخشى تركيا من تأثير إيران المتزايد في المنطقة.

articles.context: تستمر الحرب في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. النزاع الذي نشأ عن صراع على السلطة بين الجنرال عبد الفتاح البرهان وقائد قوات الدعم السريع محمد حمدان دقلو، ساهم في تدمير البنية التحتية وارتفاع معدلات النزوح، مما جعل البلاد تواجه أسوأ أزمة جوع منذ عقود.

articles.title: TIFA: Kindiki most preferred to be President Ruto's running mate
articles.summary: A new TIFA poll has handed Kindiki a major political boost ahead of 2027, placing him far ahead...
articles.context: The TIFA poll comes amid heightened political maneuvering in Kenya as President William Ruto prepares for the 2027 elections. Kindiki, currently the Interior Cabinet Secretary, has emerged as a key figure, potentially appealing to both Ruto's base and undecided voters in a polarized political landscape.

articles.title: Court to decide Matara fate in Starlet Wahu murder case
articles.summary: Matara faces make-or-break ruling next week in the Starlet Wahu murder trial.
articles.context: The murder of Starlet Wahu in 2021 sparked widespread outrage in Kenya, highlighting issues of gender-based violence and the failures of the justice system. Matara, a prominent figure in the case, has been implicated in Wahu's death, which has drawn attention from women's rights groups and activists. The upcoming ruling is critical as it may set a precedent for how similar cases are handled in the future, especially in light of the 2015 Gender-Based Violence Act.

articles.title: Court told quashing Gachagua impeachment could trigger two-Deputy Presidents crisis
articles.summary: Fresh constitutional dilemma in Gachagua impeachment petitions.
articles.context: The ongoing impeachment petitions against Kenya's Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua have emerged amidst a politically charged environment following the contentious 2022 elections, which saw President William Ruto and Gachagua's Kenya Kwanza coalition come to power. The potential quashing of these impeachment efforts raises concerns about the constitutional implications of having two Deputy Presidents, particularly in light of the 2010 Constitution that established the current political framework and the precedent set by previous leadership struggles.

articles.title: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years over corruption
articles.summary: A 68-year-old former Nigerian power minister has been sentenced to 75 years in prison for laundering $24.7m, in a rare conviction against corrupt officials in the West African nation, the BBC has reported. […] The post Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years over corruption first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The conviction of Saleh Mamman comes amid Nigeria's ongoing struggle with corruption, which has plagued its governance since the end of military rule in 1999. This case is particularly significant as it follows the 2020 establishment of Nigeria’s Special Presidential Investigation Panel, aimed at tackling high-profile corruption. Additionally, the ruling may influence public perception and political accountability in a region where corruption undermines development, especially in neighboring countries like South Sudan, which faces its own governance challenges.

articles.title: Concerns raised over unsafe reuse of bottles in markets amid cholera outbreak
articles.summary: Health officials and community members are raising alarm over poor hygiene practices, including the reuse of plastic bottles in markets, warning that this could worsen the ongoing cholera outbreak in South Sudan. Across markets in Juba, empty plastic bottles are reportedly being collected from streets and garbage sites, then reused to package and sell local […] The post Concerns raised over unsafe reuse of bottles in markets amid cholera outbreak first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with a cholera outbreak since 2021, exacerbated by ongoing conflict and displacement, which has strained healthcare resources. The 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed to stabilize the country, but persistent violence and economic instability have hindered effective public health measures. As of October 2023, the resurgence of cholera cases has raised urgent concerns about sanitation practices, particularly in Juba's markets, where the reuse of contaminated bottles poses a significant health risk.

articles.title: Why Fally Ipupa didn't perform at Kasarani as Bien, Chameleone, Yemi kept the energy alive
articles.summary: For many Kenyan fans, Fally was the headline act they had eagerly waited to see.
articles.context: Fally Ipupa's anticipated performance at the Kasarani Stadium in Nairobi was part of a broader cultural exchange between East African nations, particularly following the 2022 agreement between Kenya and the Democratic Republic of Congo to strengthen bilateral ties through music and arts. The absence of the Congolese star during this event, especially after the recent resurgence of live performances post-COVID-19, highlights the ongoing challenges artists face in navigating logistics and regional cooperation in the entertainment industry.

articles.title: سقوط 61 شخصًا في اشتباكات دامية بكاودا
articles.summary: جنوب كردفان – تحولت مدينة كاودا، المعقل الرئيسي للحركة الشعبية – شمال بقيادة عبد العزيز الحلو، إلى ساحة دامية بعد اشتباكات عنيفة مع قبيلة الأطورو، أسفرت عن مقتل 61 شخصًا بينهم نساء وأطفال، وفق ما أعلنت شبكة أطباء السودان. الشبكة أوضحت أن فريقها الميداني استمع إلى شهادات ناجين أكدوا أن المدنيين تعرضوا للاستهداف المباشر دون …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الاشتباكات في كاودا في سياق النزاع المستمر في جنوب كردفان، حيث تتصارع الحركة الشعبية – شمال بقيادة عبد العزيز الحلو مع الحكومة السودانية وقبائل محلية مثل الأطورو. منذ اندلاع النزاع في 2011، شهدت المنطقة تصاعدًا في العنف، خاصة بعد اتفاقية السلام التي أُبرمت في 2020، والتي لم تشمل جميع الأطراف المعنية، مما زاد من التوترات بين الجماعات المسلحة والمجتمعات المحلية.

articles.title: السودان يرفض تقريرًا نهائيًا قبل مقابلة الضحايا
articles.summary: وجّهت الحكومة السودانية تحذيرًا رسميًا إلى بعثة تقصي الحقائق التابعة للاتحاد الأفريقي، مطالبة بعدم إصدار تقرير نهائي بشأن الانتهاكات قبل الاستماع إلى جميع المتضررين داخل البلاد. جاء ذلك خلال لقاء وفد السودان المشارك في أعمال الدورة 78 للجنة حقوق الإنسان والشعوب الأفريقية مع رئيس البعثة ومفوض الاتحاد المعني بملف السودان، حاتم الصائم. رئيس الوفد، السفير …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التطورات في سياق تصاعد التوترات في السودان بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تتواصل الصراعات بين القوات المسلحة والدعم السريع. كما أن تقرير بعثة تقصي الحقائق التابعة للاتحاد الأفريقي يأتي في وقت حساس، حيث يسعى المجتمع الدولي إلى معالجة الانتهاكات التي وقعت خلال النزاعات الأخيرة، بما في ذلك أحداث دارفور.

articles.title: الأرصاد السودانية تحذر من موجة حر قاسية تضرب ثلاث ولايات
articles.summary: أطلقت الهيئة العامة للأرصاد الجوية في السودان تحذيرًا عاجلًا لسكان ثلاث ولايات، مشيرة إلى أن البلاد ستشهد موجة حر قاسية خلال الساعات المقبلة قد تمتد لثلاثة أيام متواصلة. ووفق الإنذار الجوي، فإن الارتفاع الكبير في درجات الحرارة سيؤثر بشكل مباشر على جنوب الولاية الشمالية، وولاية نهر النيل، إضافة إلى المناطق الغربية من ولاية البحر الأحمر، …
articles.context: تأتي تحذيرات الأرصاد الجوية في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يعاني البلد من أزمات متعددة تشمل النزاعات المسلحة في مناطق مثل دارفور، والأزمة الاقتصادية المتفاقمة منذ الإطاحة بالرئيس عمر البشير في 2019. كما أن التغيرات المناخية تؤثر بشكل متزايد على الأمن الغذائي، مما يزيد من أهمية التحذيرات المتعلقة بالطقس، خاصة في ظل الظروف الصعبة التي يواجهها السكان في الولايات المتأثرة.

In a press statement dated May 13, 2026, the ministry said it received “with […] The post Foreign ministry rejects U.S. sanctions, calls for constructive dialogue first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The U.S. sanctions come amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil war. articles.title: أول تعليق من حمزة عبد الكريم بعد تأهل برشلونة لنصف نهائي كأس الأبطال

articles.title: ماذا تعني الحرب لجيلٍ عاشها وآخر اختبرها عن بُعد؟
articles.summary: من غزة إلى السودان واليمن ولبنان... شباب من منطقة الشرق الأوسط وشمال أفريقيا يروون كيف غيّرت الحرب حياتهم، بين النزوح والفنّ والأمل والسعي إلى السلام.
articles.context: تعيش السودان منذ عام 2023 حالة من الفوضى نتيجة الصراع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى نزوح الملايين وتدمير البنية التحتية. تتزامن هذه الأحداث مع استمرار النزاعات في مناطق أخرى من الشرق الأوسط، مثل غزة واليمن، مما يسلط الضوء على تأثير الحروب المتعددة على الشباب في المنطقة. هذه التجارب المشتركة تعكس التحديات التي يواجهها الجيل الجديد في سعيه نحو السلام والأمل.

The Ethiopian Football Federation's efforts to professionalize the league further highlight the sport's potential to stimulate local economies and promote social cohesion.

articles.title: GameZone Tongits Win Like a Pro: Advanced Strategies You Need
articles.summary: What really separates a casual Tongits player from a true champion? It’s not just luck—it’s how you think, plan, and react under pressure. Every round of Tongits is more than just drawing and discarding cards. It’s a battle of strategy, awareness, and discipline. If you’ve ever wondered how top players consistently achieve a GameZone Tongits […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing tensions following the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, have led to a renewed focus on community-building activities as a means of fostering unity. As the country seeks to recover from the humanitarian crisis, games like Tongits have emerged as popular social pastimes, providing a platform for interaction and strategy development among diverse groups. This article highlights how mastering such games can reflect broader themes of resilience and strategic thinking in a challenging environment.

articles.title: Jimma Hosts 3rd “Discover Ethiopia Classics” Race, Championing Sports Tourism and Polio Eradication
articles.summary: The historic city of Jimma became a vibrant hub of athletics and public health advocacy on April 26, 2026, as it hosted the third stage of the “Discover Ethiopia Classics” race series. The event, which drew more than 4,000 participants, successfully blended the promotion of sports tourism with a critical, global mission: “END POLIO NOW.” […]
articles.context: The "Discover Ethiopia Classics" race occurs amid Ethiopia's ongoing efforts to boost tourism and economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As the country seeks to rebuild its international image, events like this race not only promote sports tourism but also align with global health initiatives, particularly the push to eradicate polio, which remains a concern in the region despite significant progress.

articles.title: INVITATION TO BID
articles.summary: Sale of Unserviceable Touchscreen Displays and UPS REF#PRO32-3-752-PMU/26-4-1 The United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA) invites interested bidders to participate in a closed bid exercise for the sale of unserviceable touchscreen displays and UPS. The bid items are to be sold “as is, where is” without recourse to warranties of any kind. Bid with […]
articles.context: The sale of unserviceable equipment by the UNECA comes at a time when Ethiopia is navigating significant economic challenges following the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022. The ongoing recovery efforts and the need for financial resources make this bid particularly relevant, as the government seeks to optimize assets and manage public resources effectively amidst a backdrop of political instability and economic reform.

articles.title: Kenya police confirm 18 deaths as heavy rains trigger landslides in multiple counties
articles.summary: Authorities warn of rising disaster risks; rescue teams respond to mudslides and flooding
articles.context: Kenya has faced increasing climate-related disasters, with heavy rains exacerbated by deforestation and poor land management practices. The recent El Niño weather pattern, which began affecting the region in late 2022, has intensified rainfall, leading to significant flooding and landslides. This disaster comes amid ongoing political tensions following the contentious 2022 presidential elections, where President William Ruto's administration has struggled to address both climate resilience and public safety concerns.

articles.title: 2,600 more civilians displaced by worsening insecurity in Sudan’s North Kordofan: UN migration agency
articles.summary: IOM says new wave of displacement reported on April 29-30
articles.context: The recent displacement in North Kordofan follows escalating violence amid Sudan's ongoing conflict, which reignited after the military coup in October 2021. The power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces has exacerbated humanitarian crises, leading to increased violence and instability. The UN's International Organization for Migration reported this latest wave of displacement on April 29-30, highlighting the urgent need for humanitarian assistance in a region already strained by previous conflicts.

articles.title: Slot Relief During the Iran Crisis: Why Flexibility Matters Now
articles.summary: How regulators can make fair, consistent decisions when war and fuel shortages disrupt normal flight operations An airport slot is critical. And at many airports there are not enough slots to fulfill the demand for travel at all times of the day. That’s why airlines, airports, and regulators worked together to create the Worldwide Airport […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly the Tigray War that began in November 2020, has severely disrupted transportation and logistics in the Horn of Africa. As the region grapples with fuel shortages exacerbated by geopolitical tensions, including the Iran crisis, the need for flexible airport slot management has become critical for humanitarian aid and commercial travel. This situation is further complicated by the Ethiopian government’s efforts to stabilize the economy while addressing security concerns.

articles.title: Aviation as an economic catalyst: Why Ethiopia must seize the moment
articles.summary: Addis Ababa was last week at the center of a continental transformation. As the host of the International Air Transport Association’s (IATA) Focus Africa conference, the city has become a hub for industry leaders, government officials, and policymakers debating the future of air travel in Africa. For Ethiopia, this conference is not merely a diplomatic […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's strategic location as a gateway to Africa has been underscored by its growing aviation sector, particularly through Ethiopian Airlines, which has expanded rapidly since its establishment in 1945. The recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2018 highlights the potential for increased regional connectivity. However, ongoing internal conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region, pose challenges to stability and economic growth, making the IATA conference a pivotal moment for Ethiopia's aviation ambitions.

articles.context: Melat Berhe's establishment of Zoma Hair Oil in Dire Dawa comes at a time when Ethiopia is navigating post-conflict recovery following the Tigray War, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. As the country seeks to revitalize its economy and promote entrepreneurship, particularly among women, Melat's venture symbolizes a shift towards local production and empowerment in a region historically impacted by conflict and economic instability.

articles.title: The Djibouti-Ethiopia corridor powering Ethiopia’s trade offers lessons for Africa
articles.summary: More than 95% of Ethiopia’s imports and exports travel along a single corridor linking Addis Ababa to the Port of Djibouti. Every truck on that road carries the weight of an economy serving more than 120 million people. What happens at those checkpoints shapes the price of food in the capital, the viability of a […]
articles.context: The Djibouti-Ethiopia corridor has been crucial since the 1998-2000 Eritrean-Ethiopian War, which cut off Ethiopia's access to Eritrean ports, making Djibouti its primary trade route. The recent Tigray conflict, which officially ended in November 2022, has further underscored the corridor's importance for stabilizing Ethiopia's economy. Additionally, the African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizes regional integration, making the efficiency of this corridor a focal point for broader economic collaboration in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Kenya battles to stop the 'goons and guns' as fears of political violence grow
articles.summary: More than 100 gangs are operating nationwide as the growing use of political thugs raises fears of election violence.
articles.context: Kenya's political landscape has been marred by violence and electoral disputes, notably during the contentious 2007-2008 elections, which resulted in over 1,000 deaths. The rise of gangs, often used by politicians as enforcers, has resurfaced ahead of the 2022 elections, exacerbating fears of unrest. The recent formation of the Azimio la Umoja coalition, led by Raila Odinga, has intensified competition with President William Ruto’s administration, further fueling tensions and the potential for violence.

articles.title: Algeria’s Fateh Boutbig Elected as President of Pan-African Parliament
articles.summary: Algeria’s Fateh Boutbig has been elected as the new President of the Pan-African Parliament (PAP), a legislative body of the African Union. The PAP eleced a new bureau
articles.context: Fateh Boutbig's election as President of the Pan-African Parliament comes at a time when the African Union is seeking to strengthen continental unity amid rising geopolitical tensions, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict in Ethiopia. His leadership may influence ongoing discussions around the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) and regional security, especially as member states grapple with issues of governance and economic recovery post-COVID-19.

articles.title: IATA: Continued Invest in Aviation Critical as Ethiopia’s Passenger Demand Triple by 2044
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – The International Air Transport Association (IATA) called on the Government to continue prioritizing aviation to sustain Ethiopia’s strong demand outlook for air travel. The airline
articles.context: Ethiopia's aviation sector has been pivotal for economic growth, especially following the establishment of Ethiopian Airlines as a leading carrier in Africa. However, ongoing conflicts, particularly in the Tigray region since late 2020, have disrupted infrastructure and investment. The Ethiopian government’s commitment to the African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizes regional connectivity, making IATA's call for continued investment in aviation crucial for stabilizing the economy and enhancing international relations amidst these challenges.

articles.title: Oil tanker hijacked off coast of Yemen and taken towards Somalia
articles.summary: It is the second time an oil tanker has been hijacked in the area in the past 10 days.
articles.context: The hijacking of the oil tanker off Yemen's coast comes amid escalating piracy incidents linked to the ongoing instability in Somalia, where militant groups like Al-Shabaab exploit the chaos. The recent surge in maritime attacks follows the collapse of the 2012 Djibouti Agreement aimed at stabilizing Somalia, highlighting the challenges of securing international shipping routes in the region. This incident marks the second hijacking in just over a week, raising concerns over maritime security.

articles.title: “Our Only Question is Development”: President Mustafe Omer’s Optimistic Take on Security, Investment, and Elections
articles.summary: An agricultural economist by training from the Imperial College of London and formerly worked at international organizations like the UN, Mustafe Mohammed Omer has served as president of the Somali Regional State since August 2018. A senior member of the ruling Prosperity Party today, Mustafe’s term in office has coincided with a period of relative […]
articles.context: Since taking office in August 2018, President Mustafe Omer has navigated the complexities of the Somali Regional State amid ongoing tensions between the federal government and regional authorities, particularly following the 2020 Ethio-Somali conflict over resource allocation. His leadership comes at a critical juncture as Ethiopia prepares for national elections in 2025, with the Prosperity Party aiming to stabilize the region and attract foreign investment to foster economic growth amidst security challenges.

articles.title: Ethiopia to Reconsider Auto Import Ban as Part of WTO Accession Efforts
articles.summary: The federal government may soon revisit its restriction on fuel-powered automobile imports as part of Ethiopia’s long-standing efforts to gain accession to the World Trade Organization (WTO). Trade Minister Kassahun Gofe (PhD), who leads Ethiopia’s negotiations with the WTO, mentioned vehicle import reconsiderations while addressing the seventh Working Party Meeting on Ethiopia’s accession earlier this […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's auto import ban, implemented in 2018 to protect local industries, has faced criticism for stifling competition and innovation. As the country navigates ongoing economic challenges and seeks to stabilize following the Tigray conflict, the government’s potential policy shift aligns with its commitment to the WTO, where accession discussions have been ongoing since 2003. This reconsideration could signal a broader move towards liberalizing the economy and attracting foreign investment.

articles.title: London Court Dismisses Bulk of Turkish Contractor’s Claims Against Ethiopia
articles.summary: The London Court of International Arbitration has dismissed the bulk of a USD 979.9 million claim filed by Turkish company Yapi Merkezi Insaat against the Ethiopian Railway Corporation (ERC), according to sources familiar with the case. The company had sought compensation for damages and losses incurred during the construction of the Awash–Kombolcha–Woldia–Hara Gebeya railway infrastructure […]
articles.context: The dispute between Yapi Merkezi Insaat and the Ethiopian Railway Corporation stems from the broader context of Ethiopia's ambitious infrastructure development plans, particularly the 2010 Growth and Transformation Plan aimed at boosting economic growth. The Awash–Kombolcha–Woldia railway project, part of this initiative, has faced delays and funding challenges, exacerbated by ongoing political tensions and conflict in the Tigray region, which has affected logistics and investment in the country since late 2020.

articles.title: INSA Inks Proclamation to Bolster Cybersecurity Defenses
articles.summary: Draft proposes third-party cyber security service providers, under review in Parliament Experts at the Information Network Security Administration (INSA) have tabled a draft proclamation as a framework to guide efforts to curb increasingly frequent cyber threats on Ethiopia’s growing digital visibility of sensitive national data and information. The ‘Critical Infrastructure Cybersecurity Proclamation,’ which has been […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced escalating cyber threats amid its ongoing political unrest, particularly following the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The government's push for digital transformation, including the expansion of online services, has heightened vulnerabilities to cyberattacks. The proposed proclamation comes as the country seeks to strengthen its cybersecurity framework in response to incidents like the 2021 cyberattack on the Ethiopian Electric Power Corporation, which compromised critical infrastructure.

articles.title: From Hardship to Hazard: Tackling Ethiopia’s Migration Pressures
articles.summary: The tragic reports that 65 Ethiopian migrants are at imminent risk of execution in Saudi Arabia, following the recent execution of three others alongside the killing of four Ethiopian nationals in South Africa amid rising xenophobic tensions, are a sobering reminder of the human cost of irregular migration. These incidents highlight the vulnerability of Ethiopians […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant upheaval since the outbreak of the Tigray conflict in November 2020, which has exacerbated economic instability and human rights abuses. The ongoing political tensions, coupled with a deteriorating security situation, have driven many Ethiopians to seek refuge abroad, often through perilous migration routes. Recent xenophobic violence in South Africa and harsh crackdowns on migrants in Saudi Arabia underscore the escalating dangers faced by those fleeing Ethiopia's turmoil.

articles.title: Surging Expenditures Push Mid-Year Budget Deficit Over 90bln Birr
articles.summary: The federal government posted a budget deficit of 93.6 billion Birr in the first half of the 2025/26 fiscal year (EFY 2018), as a sharp rise in public spending outpaced strong revenue growth, according to the latest mid-year budget implementation review released by the Ministry of Finance. The report shows that while total revenues and […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is currently navigating a complex political landscape marked by the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has been increasing public spending to support reconstruction and development efforts, yet this surge in expenditures has led to significant budget deficits, raising concerns about fiscal sustainability amid ongoing economic challenges and regional tensions.

articles.title: Rights Group Warns of Imminent Execution Risk of Ethiopian Migrants in Saudi Arabia
articles.summary: Human Rights Watch (HRW) warned on Tuesday that Saudi Arabian authorities are moving to execute at least 65 Ethiopian migrants following convictions for drug-related offenses. In an urgent appeal for international intervention, the rights organization stressed that the lives of these individuals remain at immediate risk unless diplomatic pressure halts the judicial proceedings. The group […]
articles.context: The warning from Human Rights Watch comes amid heightened tensions between Ethiopia and Saudi Arabia, following a surge in Ethiopian migration to the Gulf states due to ongoing conflict and instability in Ethiopia, particularly since the Tigray War began in November 2020. The Ethiopian government has faced criticism for its handling of the conflict, leading many to seek better opportunities abroad, often resulting in precarious situations for migrants in countries like Saudi Arabia.

articles.title: New Health Directive Holds Medical Agents Liable for Foreign Malpractice
articles.summary: A new directive issued by the Ministry of Health is reshaping the country’s medical travel landscape by placing legal responsibility on third-party agents who arrange treatment abroad. The Overseas Medical Service Referral System Implementation Directive introduces sweeping changes to how Ethiopian patients are referred to and treated in foreign healthcare facilities. Most notably, it establishes an […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's healthcare system has faced significant challenges, exacerbated by the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020, which disrupted medical services and access to care. The new directive, issued in October 2023, aims to regulate medical travel amid rising concerns over malpractice and patient safety, particularly as many Ethiopians seek treatment abroad due to inadequate local facilities. This shift reflects the government's attempt to enhance accountability in the medical referral process.

articles.title: Ethio telecom in Talks with UK Development Finance Institution
articles.summary: This week, Ethio telecom announced the “successful conclusion” of talks with British International Investment (BII), the UK government’s development finance institution handling a portfolio valued at over seven billion dollars in emerging markets in Africa, Asia, and the Caribbean. articles.context: Ethio telecom's discussions with British International Investment come at a critical time as Ethiopia seeks to attract foreign investment amid ongoing recovery from the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government, under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, is prioritizing economic reforms and infrastructure development to stabilize the economy and foster international partnerships, making this engagement with a UK institution particularly significant for future growth prospects.

articles.title: Africa receives only 11pct of annual climate finance need: UNECA
articles.summary: ‘COP32 must be a turning point’ Africa is receiving only 11 percent of the USD277 billion climate finance required annually until 2030. Although Africa contributes less than four percent of global greenhouse gas emissions, it remains among the region’s most vulnerable to climate change, according to latest statement from UNECA. African climate leaders, negotiators, policymakers […]
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Ethiopia, particularly the Tigray War that escalated in November 2020, has exacerbated the country's vulnerability to climate change, hindering its capacity to adapt and respond to environmental challenges. With the African Union's Agenda 2063 emphasizing sustainable development, the lack of adequate climate finance undermines regional efforts to achieve resilience and stability. The upcoming COP32 in 2024 presents a critical opportunity for African leaders to advocate for increased funding and support.

articles.title: FSD Ethiopia CEO Hikmet Joins INPRF Board to Shape Global NPO Reporting
articles.summary: The International Non-Profit Reporting Foundation (INPRF), formerly known as IFR4NPO, has announced the appointment of Hikmet Abdella, CEO of Financial Sector Deepening (FSD) Ethiopia, as a member of its board. This appointment brings a prominent Ethiopian expert to the global stage as the international foundation works to implement the first-ever International Non-Profit Accounting Guidance (INPAG), […]
articles.context: Hikmet Abdella's appointment to the INPRF board comes at a crucial time for Ethiopia, which has been grappling with political instability and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. As the country seeks to rebuild its economy and enhance transparency in the non-profit sector, Abdella's expertise could play a vital role in shaping international standards that align with Ethiopia's development goals and foster trust among donors and stakeholders.

articles.title: From Import Debt to Energy Sovereignty: The Macroeconomics of Ethiopia’s EV Revolution
articles.summary: The quiet hum of electric motors on the streets of Addis Ababa is more than a trend. It is the sound of a country decoupling its future from the volatility of global oil markets. It is the outcome of a decade-long strategic architecture. At its core is the Climate Resilient Green Economy (CRGE) strategy. This strategy identified early on that if the transport sector is […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's push towards electric vehicles (EVs) comes amid ongoing tensions from the Tigray conflict, which has strained the economy and highlighted the need for energy independence. The government's Climate Resilient Green Economy strategy, launched in 2011, aims to transition the nation to a carbon-neutral economy by 2025. With rising oil prices and a commitment to sustainable development, Ethiopia's EV revolution is a critical step towards economic resilience and energy sovereignty.

articles.title: ارتفاع معدلات السرطان بين الشباب: هل تفسّر السمنة جزءاً من اللغز؟
articles.summary: يشدد الباحثون على أن تغييرات بسيطة في نمط الحياة لا تزال قادرة على خفض خطر الإصابة بالسرطان بدرجة كبيرة.
articles.context: تشهد الصومال ارتفاعًا ملحوظًا في معدلات الإصابة بالسرطان بين الشباب، مما يسلط الضوء على تأثير الأزمات الصحية الناتجة عن الصراع المستمر منذ عقود. يعاني النظام الصحي من ضعف الموارد، مما يجعل الوصول إلى الرعاية الصحية والتوعية حول العوامل المسببة للسرطان تحديًا كبيرًا. في ظل جهود الحكومة الانتقالية الحالية لتعزيز الخدمات الصحية، تبرز أهمية معالجة السمنة كعامل خطر رئيسي في مكافحة الأمراض غير المعدية.

articles.title: Dashen Breweries Appoints Matyas Getachew as New CEO
articles.summary: Dashen Breweries SC has appointed Matyas Getachew as its new Chief Executive Officer with effect from Monday, 27 April 2026. The Brewery announced the end of its search
articles.context: Dashen Breweries' appointment of Matyas Getachew comes at a critical time as Ethiopia navigates post-conflict recovery following the Tigray War, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The brewing industry has faced significant challenges due to economic instability and supply chain disruptions, making strong leadership essential for revitalizing operations and fostering growth in a competitive market. Getachew's experience will be pivotal in steering the company through these turbulent times.

articles.title: March Passenger Demand Up 2.1% but Sharp Regional Differences
articles.summary: Global passenger traffic grew by 2.1% Year-on-Year in March, a sharp slowdown from the 6.1% increase in February and the weakest post‑pandemic growth to date, the International Air
articles.context: In March 2023, Ethiopia was navigating the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022. The peace deal aimed to stabilize the region and revitalize the economy, but ongoing tensions and humanitarian challenges have impacted recovery efforts. As Ethiopia seeks to boost tourism and air travel, the modest growth in passenger demand highlights the uneven recovery across the Horn of Africa, where political stability remains fragile.

articles.title: UN Security Council extends South Sudan mission mandate for 1 year
articles.summary: On US resolution, 13 council members vote to extend mission until April 30, 2027; Russia, China abstain
articles.context: The UN Security Council's decision to extend the mandate of the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) comes amid ongoing violence and instability in the country, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan. Despite the peace deal, clashes between government forces and opposition groups persist, exacerbating humanitarian crises. The abstention of Russia and China highlights geopolitical tensions and differing approaches to intervention in the region.

articles.title: "الانتحار خوفاً من الاغتصاب": تحذيرات دولية من تبعات العنف الجنسي في السودان
articles.summary: حذرت وكالات تابعة للأمم المتحدة ومنظمات غير حكومية محلية ودولية من التبعات الخطيرة لاستخدام الاغتصاب وغيره من أشكال العنف الجنسي على نطاق واسع في السودان، ولا سيما على الصحة النفسية للضحايا والناجيات.
articles.context: تشهد السودان منذ عام 2019 صراعًا سياسيًا وأمنيًا متصاعدًا بعد الإطاحة بنظام عمر البشير، مما أدى إلى تفشي العنف والفوضى. في يونيو 2023، اندلعت اشتباكات مسلحة بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما زاد من حالات العنف الجنسي. هذه الظروف أدت إلى تحذيرات من الأمم المتحدة بشأن التأثيرات النفسية والاجتماعية العميقة على النساء والفتيات في البلاد، حيث أصبح الانتحار خيارًا مأساويًا للكثيرات.

articles.title: Binance Announces Suspension of Crypto Trading Services Birr
articles.summary: The world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange, Binance, has officially announced that it will suspend Peer-to-Peer (P2P) trading services involving the Ethiopian Birr effective May 15, 2026. The platform disclosed that this decision follows mounting pressure from the country’s financial regulatory bodies, marking a significant shift in Ethiopia’s digital currency landscape. This suspension comes just two months […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a complex political landscape, including ongoing tensions between the federal government and Tigrayan forces since the civil conflict erupted in November 2020. The National Bank of Ethiopia has been tightening regulations on digital currencies to curb potential financial instability, particularly following the 2021 introduction of a new digital currency framework. Binance's suspension of P2P trading in Birr highlights the increasing influence of regulatory bodies amid these turbulent economic conditions.

articles.title: Meta in row after workers in Kenya who say they saw smart glasses users having sex lose jobs
articles.summary: Meta and its subcontractor disagree over why over 1000 Kenya-based workers were made redundant.
articles.context: The controversy arises amid ongoing scrutiny of Meta's labor practices in Kenya, where the company has faced criticism for the treatment of its content moderators. In 2022, a report by the International Labor Organization highlighted the precarious working conditions for these subcontracted workers. The recent layoffs, reportedly linked to incidents involving smart glasses, have reignited debates about worker rights and corporate accountability in the tech industry, particularly in a region increasingly pivotal to digital innovation.

articles.title: 'I did not expect it': Kenya's Sabastian Sawe welcomed home with jubilant celebrations
articles.summary: The first man to run a marathon in under two hours in a competitive race received a hero's welcome from supporters and family when he arrived home.
articles.context: Kenya has a rich history of long-distance running, producing world-class athletes like Eliud Kipchoge, who first broke the two-hour marathon barrier in a non-competitive setting in 2019. Sabastian Sawe's achievement comes at a time when the country is grappling with economic challenges and political tensions, particularly following the contentious 2022 elections. His success not only boosts national pride but also serves as a unifying moment for Kenyans amid ongoing socio-political divisions.

articles.title: Joy as record-breaking runner Sawe returns home
articles.summary: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.
articles.context: Sebastian Sawe's achievement comes at a time when Kenya is grappling with political unrest following the controversial 2022 presidential elections, which saw President William Ruto take office amid allegations of electoral fraud. This victory in sports not only unites Kenyans in national pride but also serves as a welcome distraction from ongoing economic challenges and political tensions, fostering a sense of hope and resilience in the face of adversity.

articles.title: Awash Bank Officially Listed on Ethiopian Securities Exchange
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) announced the official listing of Awash Bank S.C. on its main market, marking a historic milestone in the continued development of
articles.context: The listing of Awash Bank on the Ethiopian Securities Exchange comes amid ongoing economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed at liberalizing Ethiopia's economy. This move is significant as it reflects the government's efforts to attract foreign investment and stabilize the economy, particularly in the wake of the Tigray conflict that has strained financial resources and investor confidence since its outbreak in November 2020.

articles.title: Endangered antelopes flown to Kenya from Czech Republic in 'historic homecoming'
articles.summary: The mountain bongo is a rare antelope endemic to Kenyan highland forests, with less than 100 in the wild.
articles.context: The mountain bongo, once widespread in Kenya's highland forests, has faced severe population declines due to habitat loss and poaching, leading to its classification as critically endangered. Conservation efforts intensified after the 2019 agreement between the Kenya Wildlife Service and international partners to restore the species. The recent flight of bongos from the Czech Republic marks a significant step in these efforts, highlighting ongoing global collaboration to revive Kenya's unique biodiversity amid increasing environmental pressures.

articles.title: Kenya's leader backtracks after comments mocking Nigerians' English
articles.summary: He says his words were intended to be a private conversation and were "taken out of context".
articles.context: Kenya's President William Ruto made the controversial remarks during a private meeting on October 10, 2023, which sparked outrage in Nigeria and among the broader African community. This incident comes amid ongoing efforts to strengthen regional ties through initiatives like the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA), highlighting the sensitivity of inter-African relations and the importance of diplomatic language in fostering unity. Ruto's comments risk undermining these collaborative efforts.

articles.title: New draft proclamation opens insurance sector to foreign investment
articles.summary: Ethiopia officially plans to open its insurance sector to foreign investment through a newly drafted Insurance Proclamation. This move marks a fundamental shift in the financial landscape, ending decades of market dominance held exclusively by domestic institutions. The proposed draft legislation includes broad reforms designed to modernize the sector, strengthen financial stability, and align Ethiopia’s […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's decision to open its insurance sector to foreign investment comes amid ongoing economic reforms initiated by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed since 2018, aimed at liberalizing the economy. This shift follows the 2020 signing of the Abiy-led Prosperity Party's agenda, which emphasizes privatization and foreign investment. The move is particularly significant as Ethiopia navigates internal conflicts, including the Tigray War, and seeks to stabilize its economy while attracting international capital.

articles.title: 'I was not thinking to run a world record'
articles.summary: Kenya's new sporting icon Sabastian Sawe admits his world record time at the London Marathon came as a surprise.
articles.context: Kenya has a rich history of long-distance running dominance, with athletes like Eliud Kipchoge setting world records and winning multiple marathons. Sawe's unexpected achievement at the London Marathon on April 23, 2023, highlights the country's continued emergence of new talent amidst a competitive landscape. This success comes as Kenya grapples with internal challenges, including political tensions and economic struggles, making Sawe's triumph a unifying moment for the nation.

articles.title: Suspects in Scot's murder in Kenya charged over attack on another man
articles.summary: The four men pleaded not guilty to targeting an American in an alleged attack at a holiday apartment.
articles.context: The murder of the Scottish national in Kenya has heightened concerns about security for expatriates and tourists, particularly in popular regions like Nairobi and the coastal areas. This incident follows a series of violent crimes against foreigners, prompting the Kenyan government to enhance security measures. The ongoing challenges of crime and tourism safety are critical as Kenya seeks to recover from the economic impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic and restore international confidence.

articles.title: Plane crash in South Sudan kills all 15 on board
articles.summary: The authorities are investigating but early reports suggest bad weather and poor visibility may be to blame.
articles.context: The plane crash in South Sudan occurs amidst ongoing instability following the civil war that lasted from 2013 to 2018, which left the country deeply divided. The peace agreement signed in September 2018 has seen fragile progress, but tensions remain high, particularly with the recent clashes in Unity State. articles.summary: الخرطوم 20 مارس 2026 – تثير الحرب على إيران الدائرة منذ نحو ثلاثة أسابيع مخاوف من انعكاسات خطيرة على إمدادات الأدوية وارتفاع تكاليف التصنيع والاستيراد، وسط توقعات بتفاقم آثارها على السودان ما اضطر السلطات للاستعداد لأسواء الاحتمالات. ويعاني السودان أصلا من تدمير قطاع التصنيع الدوائي وشح النقد الأجنبي. وتضرر نحو 85% من القطاع الصناعي في السودان جراء الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع منذ 2023، بما في ذلك مصانع الأدوية بولاية الخرطوم، التي كانت تنتج نحو 80 صنفا دوائيا، حيث تعرض بعضها للتدمير، بينما توقف إنتاج البعض الآخر بسبب انعدام الأمن، وفقًا لإحصاءات رسمية. وعقد وزير الصحة السوداني هيثم محمد إبراهيم، الأسبوع الماضي، الاجتماع...

articles.title: قائد الدعم السريع يجدد القبول بهدنة إنسانية «جادة»
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 20 مارس 2026 – جدد قائد قوات الدعم السريع محمد حمدان دقلو حميدتي، الجمعة، موافقته الصريحة على هدنة جادة وذات مصداقية للأغراض الإنسانية الخالصة. وقال حميدتي في كلمة له بمناسبة عيد الفطر إن الهدنة الإنسانية يجب أن تكفل وصول المساعدات إلى المدنيين بلا عوائق، وفتح الممرات الآمنة وتخفيف معاناة المتضررين وحماية النازحين واللاجئين. وأضاف قائد الدعم السريع أن تنفيذ الهدنة يجب ان يضمن عدم استغلالها لمآرب عسكرية أو سياسية ضيقة – حسب تعبيره -. وكان قائد الجيش السوداني عبد الفتاح البرهان قد جدد أمس رفضه لأي هدنة أو وقف إطلاق نار قبل أن تتجمع قوات الدعم السريع في مناطق بعينها وتسلم أسلحتها.

articles.title: البرهان يجدد شروطه للقبول بأي مبادرة سلام مع «الدعم السريع»
articles.summary: الخرطوم 19 مارس 2026 – قال قائد الجيش السوداني ورئيس مجلس السيادة عبد الفتاح البرهان، الخميس، إنه يمكن التعاطي مع أي مبادرة سلام مع قوات الدعم السريع وفقا لعدة شروط. وأكد البرهان في خطاب وجهه للسودانيين بمناسبة عيد الفطر ليل الخميس، أن قيادة الدولة حريصةً على التعاطي الإيجابي والمسؤول مع كل مبادرةٍ صادقة لسلام، لكن بما يحقق الأمن ولا يُعيد إنتاج الحرب ولا يُبقي على أسبابها أو أدواتها. وأضاف البرهان أن على الوسطاء والساعون إلى إنهاء الحرب النهوض بمسؤولياتهم والمساعدة في إنفاذ مبادرات السلام التي قدّمتها الحكومة السودانية. وتابع قائلا: “إن مبادراتٌ الحكومة تنطلق من إرادة السودانيين وتستجيب لمطلبهم العادل في تفكيك وإنهاء أي دورٍ سياسي أو...

articles.title: مسيّرات الجيش تقطع «شريان» الإمداد وتغير قواعد المعارك في كردفان
articles.summary: الأبيض 19 مارس 2026- كشفت مصادر عسكرية سودانية لـ “سودان تربيون” عن استخدام الجيش مئات المسيرات الانقضاضية (Onslaught Drones) في معارك كردفان ضد قوات الدعم السريع، مما شكل تفوقاً جوياً كاسحاً وأحدث تغييرات ملموسة في الميدان. وقالت المصادر إن الضربة الأقسى التي تلقتها قوات الدعم السريع لم تكن في ساحة المعركة فحسب، بل في “شريان الحياة” الحدودي للإمداد. وكانت “سودان تربيون” نقلت عن مصادر عسكرية الأسبوع الماضي تدمير موقعين مهمين لإمداد الدعم السريع على الحدود السودانية مع تشاد وليبيا. وشملت تيك العمليتان تنفيذ نحو 21 ضربة استباقية لإمدادات كانت في طريقها للبلاد عبر الحدود الشمالية الغربية والغربية.

articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: «أدري».. من شريان حياة إلى بوابة مؤصدة: جحيم الحصار يطارد نازحي دارفور
articles.summary: أدري 18 مارس 2026- أصدرت الحكومة التشادية في فبراير الماضي قراراً فاجأ آلاف النازحين السودانيين العالقين على حدودها الشرقية، بإعلانها إغلاق جميع المعابر الحدودية مع السودان “حتى إشعار آخر”. وكان منفذ “أدري”، الذي يعد الشريان الرئيسي الذي يعبره الفارون من جحيم الحرب في دارفور، أول المغلَقين. لم يكن القرار تشادياً خالصاً، بل جاء تتويجاً لأسابيع من التوتر المتصاعد على الحدود، أدت إلى سقوط قتلى في صفوف الجنود التشاديين. وبين ليلة وضحاها، تحول معبر الأمل إلى بوابة مؤصدة، تاركاً مئات العائلات عالقة في العراء، بين جحيم الحرب خلفها وبوابة مغلقة أمامها.

articles.summary: طويلة 18 مارس 2026 – أعلنت منظمة أنقذوا الأطفال، الأربعاء، عن إيصال أدوية لدعم 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ لمدة 6 أشهر في طويلة بولاية شمال دارفور غربي السودان. وتحوّلت طويلة، الخاضعة لسيطرة حركة تحرير السودان بقيادة عبد الواحد نور، من بلدات نائية تفتقر إلى الخدمات إلى مأوى لأكثر من 715 ألف نازح، معظمهم فرّوا من الهجمات على الفاشر ومخيمات النزوح المجاورة لها. وقالت المنظمة، في بيان، إنها أوصلت “شحنة من بورتسودان إلى طويلة لدعم أكثر من 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ في طويلة، حيث إنها كافية للإبقاء على عمل 20 مرفقًا صحيًا وعيادات متنقلة تخدم المجتمعات النازحة لمدة ستة أشهر”. وأشارت إلى أن الشحنة، التي تحمل 30 طنًا من الأدوية وإمدادات التغذية، وصلت...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.title: اتهامات للدعم السريع بإحراق قافلة محاصيل غرب النهود
articles.summary: النهود 17 مارس 2026- أقدمت قوات الدعم السريع، الثلاثاء، على إحراق قافلة تجارية تضم شاحنات كبيرة محملة بمحاصيل زراعية بالقرب من مدينة النهود بولاية غرب كردفان، أثناء توجهها إلى الأبيض بولاية شمال كردفان. ووثق عناصر من القوات عبر مقطع فيديو لحظة إضرام النار في الشاحنات التي كانت محملة بكميات كبيرة من المحاصيل الزراعية، بما في ذلك الذرة والفول السوداني. وتُعد النهود وغبيش وودبندة بولاية غرب كردفان من أبرز مناطق إنتاج الفول في السودان، كما تضم النهود واحدة من أكبر بورصات تداول المحاصيل في غرب السودان، غير أنها تعرضت لعمليات نهب واسعة عقب سيطرة الدعم السريع على المنطقة، الأمر الذي أدى إلى اضطراب النشاط التجاري ودفع العديد من التجار إلى مغادرة...

articles.title: السودان : معدل التضخم يواصل التراجع
articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.title: السلطات السودانية تعتقل القيادي الإسلامي الناجي عبد الله
articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – اعتقلت السلطات في السودان، الأحد، القيادي الإسلامي البارز الناجي عبد الله، وفقاً لما أفاد به أحد قادة المقاومة الشعبية. وظل الناجي عبد الله وهو أحد قيادات المقاومة الشعبية التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش في حربه ضد قوات الدعم السريع يجاهر بمواقفه المؤيدة للقضية الفلسطينية وداعماً لحركة المقاومة الإسلامية “حماس”. وأعلن الناجي في الثالث من مارس الجاري، خلال مخاطبته إفطاراً رمضانياً أمام حشد من مجموعات عسكرية محسوبة على التيار الإسلامي، دعمهم لإيران في الحرب التي تقودها ضد إسرائيل والولايات المتحدة الأميركية. وأثارت تلك التصريحات ردود فعل واسعة حيث أعلن القائد العام للجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان تبرؤه من تلك المجموعة، مؤكداً...

articles.title: «كبر» يكذب صحة مقطع فيديو يتحدث عن «البرهان» أثار جدلًا واسعًا
articles.summary: الخرطوم 16 مارس 2026 – نفى رئيس شورى المؤتمر الوطني – المحلول، والي شمال دارفور الأسبق عثمان محمد يوسف كبر، فجر الاثنين، صحة مقطع فيديو أثار جدلًا واسعًا في السودان تحدث خلاله عن رئيس مجلس السيادة وقائد الجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان. ونشرت قناة “اسكاي نيوز عربية” الإماراتية، الأحد، تقريرًا حوى مقطع فيديو يظهر كبر جالسًا على كرسي مريح يتحدث إلى أشخاص قربه في جلسة يبدو أنها غير رسمية، دون أن تُظهر اللقطات هؤلاء الأشخاص الجالسين قربه. وقال كبر، في مقطع الفيديو، إنهم “تحدثوا للبرهان لتسلميه المجلس العسكري لسحق المتظاهرين وتسليم السلطة إلى الإسلاميين، وهو لم يكن له دور، وما كان محسوبًا ضابطًا، حيث كان يجلس مثل الحرس خارج الباب”. وقال كبر، في بيان،...

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.
